requiredExam.label: Inspect neck for acanthosis

requiredExam.action: Use this maneuver to evaluate insulin resistance or cardiometabolic risk phenotype. Acanthosis supports insulin resistance and prompts metabolic risk counseling.
requiredExam.rationale: Assesses insulin-resistance phenotype and cardiometabolic risk so a positive finding can support diabetes/metabolic screening, counseling intensity, and risk-factor management for Prediabetes.
requiredExam.technique: Inspect the named area directly, comparing sides when relevant and documenting presence, absence, severity, and inability to assess.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: When insulin resistance or metabolic syndrome is suspected
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: insulin resistance or cardiometabolic risk phenotype
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
requiredExam.management_change: Acanthosis supports insulin resistance and prompts metabolic risk counseling.
requiredExam.limitations: Inspect neck for acanthosis is supportive for Prediabetes, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.

reference_values: Fasting plasma glucose: normal <100 mg/dL; prediabetes 100-125; diabetes >=126.
Random plasma glucose >=200 mg/dL with classic symptoms supports diabetes diagnosis.
Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects.
decision_steps: Classify prediabetes with A1c 5.7-6.4%, fasting glucose 100-125 mg/dL, or 2-hour OGTT 140-199 mg/dL after excluding diabetes-range results.
Assess BMI, waist, BP, lipids, fatty liver, sleep apnea, PCOS, prior gestational diabetes, medications, and ASCVD risk.
Repeat diabetes testing on a surveillance interval and sooner if symptoms, steroid exposure, pregnancy, or rising risk appears.
If any confirmatory result reaches diabetes range, switch to the diabetes diagnostic and treatment pathway.
Confirm that Prediabetes is the selected clinical intent, then screen for red flags before routine outpatient testing.
Order the first-line tests in sequence: A1c, fasting plasma glucose, or 2-hour 75-g OGTT; Lipids, ALT if fatty liver risk, BP/BMI/waist.

Interpret numeric results against the workup cutoffs and local assay ranges: A1c: normal <5.7%; prediabetes 5.7-6.4%; diabetes >=6.5%. Fasting plasma glucose: normal <100 mg/dL; prediabetes 100-125; diabetes >=126.

If the initial result is equivocal, discordant with the phenotype, or affected by medication, acute illness, pregnancy, age, sex, or assay interference, repeat or confirm using the next guideline test before committing to long-term therapy.
treatment_options: Offer intensive lifestyle intervention aiming for durable weight loss when appropriate, nutrition quality, physical activity, sleep, and tobacco risk reduction.
Consider metformin for higher-risk adults such as younger age with obesity, prior gestational diabetes, rising A1c, or other strong diabetes-risk features.
Treat BP, lipids, fatty liver risk, sleep apnea, and weight-related comorbidities as part of cardiometabolic risk reduction.
Use shared decision-making for anti-obesity pharmacotherapy or bariatric referral when BMI/comorbidity criteria and patient goals support it.
Treat unstable presentations first using the urgent escalation rule for Prediabetes; draw time-sensitive labs only if doing so does not delay stabilization.
For confirmed Prediabetes, choose therapy based on severity, comorbidities, pregnancy/fertility context, contraindications, patient preference, and local specialty pathway.
Monitor treatment response with the condition-specific clinical endpoint plus the most relevant labs or imaging listed in this workup.
Escalate to endocrinology or the relevant specialty when results are severe, discordant, recurrent, treatment-limiting, or require localization, surgery, genetic testing, or high-risk medication.

Prediabetes: diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup
Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: A1c: normal <5.7%; prediabetes 5.7-6.4%; diabetes >=6.5%. Fasting plasma glucose: normal <100 mg/dL; prediabetes 100-125; diabetes >=126.

Important mimics/exclusions: stress or steroid hyperglycemia, type 1/LADA versus type 2 diabetes, DKA/HHS versus uncomplicated hyperglycemia, hypoglycemia/drug effect, renal disease, infection, pregnancy, and secondary endocrine causes.
A1c 5.7-6.4, FPG 100-125, or 2-hour OGTT 140-199 triggers prevention and surveillance.
Markedly abnormal glucose, severe hypertension, pregnancy-context concern, or cardiopulmonary symptoms changes disposition from prevention visit to urgent evaluation.
Discordant or borderline endocrine tests should be confirmed and interpreted with timing, assay, medication, pregnancy, and acute illness context before irreversible treatment.

root.description: Activate when structured or extractable context matches Prediabetes triggers or the clinician selects workup prediabetes_v1.

endpoint_missing_context.description: Route here when the patient context needed to activate Prediabetes is absent or cannot be extracted.

prediabetes_concurrent_data_bundle.description: Obtain Prediabetes diagnostic/severity/treatment-safety data together rather than as a one-step chain.

Random plasma glucose >=200 mg/dL with classic symptoms supports diabetes diagnosis. root.label: Prediabetes: does patient context support this workup or require exact missing-data routing?

criteria.description: Activate when structured or extractable context matches Prediabetes triggers or the clinician selects workup prediabetes_v1.

root.action: Route Prediabetes using cited diagnostic thresholds/rules, concurrent data bundles, treatment-safety checks, monitoring, de-escalation, follow-up, and safety-net endpoints.

children.label: Missing data needed: Prediabetes pathway context
children.edgeLabel: Missing exact Prediabetes pathway context: symptoms, exam, vitals, labs/results, medications, comorbidities, demographics, pregnancy/applicability, and workup findings

criteria.description: Route here when the patient context needed to activate Prediabetes is absent or cannot be extracted.

children.action: Before choosing a non-emergency Prediabetes branch, document: presenting trigger, onset/trajectory, focused exam, vital signs, threshold labs/results, medication/allergy context, comorbidities, demographics, pregnancy/applicability status, and current workup findings.

children.label: Prediabetes: obtain concurrent diagnostic, severity, and treatment-safety data
children.edgeLabel: Selected Prediabetes workup or documented findings: How has weight changed from baseline, and was the change intentional or associated with appetite, fluid status, or systemic symptoms?; What is the usual physical activity level, and has it changed recently because of symptoms, function, or treatment barriers?; Any family history of type 2 diabetes, gestational diabetes, premature ASCVD, dyslipidemia, hypertension, or obesity that changes cardiometabolic risk?; Any history of prior gestational diabetes that changes Prediabetes risk, test interpretation, or treatment safety?; Diabetes-range glycemia or hyperglycemic symptoms.; Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation.; A1c, fasting plasma glucose, or 2-hour 75-g OGTT.

criteria.description: Obtain Prediabetes diagnostic/severity/treatment-safety data together rather than as a one-step chain.

children.action: Obtain together: A1c, fasting plasma glucose, or 2-hour 75-g OGTT.; Lipids, ALT if fatty liver risk, BP/BMI/waist.; Compare with prior results and repeat discordant nonurgent endocrine tests using correct timing, local assay, and interference precautions.; Cardiometabolic safety data: confirm A1c/FPG/OGTT classification, document BP/waist/BMI, and check lipids, kidney function, albuminuria when diabetes-range, and liver/fatty-liver context when treatment selection or risk reduction is in scope.; A1c or plasma glucose criteria for prediabetes/diabetes classification, with repeat confirmatory testing when asymptomatic or discordant.; Lipids, blood pressure, kidney function, albuminuria when diabetes-range, and liver context when cardiometabolic risk reduction or medication choice is in scope.; A1c: normal <5.7%; prediabetes 5.7-6.4%; diabetes >=6.5%.; Fasting plasma glucose: normal <100 mg/dL; prediabetes 100-125; diabetes >=126.; Measure blood pressure; Measure heart rate; Measure current weight; Measure waist circumference; Document mental status; normal <5.7%; 5.7-6.4%; diabetes >=6.5%; normal <100 mg/dL; diabetes >=126; normal <5.7%; 5.7-6.4%; diabetes >=6.5%.

criteria_options.criteria_text: Compare with prior results and repeat discordant nonurgent endocrine tests using correct timing, local assay, and interference precautions. Fasting plasma glucose: normal <100 mg/dL; prediabetes 100-125; diabetes >=126. 2-hour 75-g OGTT: normal <140 mg/dL; prediabetes 140-199; diabetes >=200. Random plasma glucose >=200 mg/dL with classic symptoms supports diabetes diagnosis. Offer intensive lifestyle intervention aiming for durable weight loss when appropriate, nutrition quality, physical activity, sleep, and tobacco risk reduction.; Consider metformin for higher-risk adults such as younger age with obesity, prior gestational diabetes, rising A1c, or other strong diabetes-risk features.; Treat BP, lipids, fatty liver risk, sleep apnea, and weight-related comorbidities as part of cardiometabolic risk reduction.; Use shared decision-making for anti-obesity pharmacotherapy or bariatric referral when BMI/comorbidity criteria and patient goals support it.; A1c 5.7-6.4, FPG 100-125, or 2-hour OGTT 140-199 triggers prevention and surveillance.; Diabetes-range values change diagnosis.; Markedly abnormal glucose, severe hypertension, pregnancy-context concern, or cardiopulmonary symptoms changes disposition from prevention visit to urgent evaluation.

parallel_actions: Offer intensive lifestyle intervention aiming for durable weight loss when appropriate, nutrition quality, physical activity, sleep, and tobacco risk reduction.
Consider metformin for higher-risk adults such as younger age with obesity, prior gestational diabetes, rising A1c, or other strong diabetes-risk features.
Treat BP, lipids, fatty liver risk, sleep apnea, and weight-related comorbidities as part of cardiometabolic risk reduction.
Use shared decision-making for anti-obesity pharmacotherapy or bariatric referral when BMI/comorbidity criteria and patient goals support it.
Treat unstable presentations first using the urgent escalation rule for Prediabetes; draw time-sensitive labs only if doing so does not delay stabilization.
A1c 5.7-6.4, FPG 100-125, or 2-hour OGTT 140-199 triggers prevention and surveillance.
Markedly abnormal glucose, severe hypertension, pregnancy-context concern, or cardiopulmonary symptoms changes disposition from prevention visit to urgent evaluation.

Prediabetes danger feature or high-risk cutoff: Diabetes-range glycemia or hyperglycemic symptoms.; Hemodynamic instability, altered mental status, severe electrolyte/glucose abnormality, arrhythmia, airway compromise, or vision threat requires urgent escalation.; Diabetes-range glycemia with classic symptoms, severe hypertension, ASCVD symptoms, pregnancy-context hyperglycemia, or dehydration requires escalation beyond routine prevention care.; Measure blood pressure; normal <5.7%; 5.7-6.4%; diabetes >=6.5%; A1c 5.7-6.4, FPG 100-125, or 2-hour OGTT 140-199 triggers prevention and surveillance.

Prediabetes mimic/exclusion better fits: Prediabetes: diagnostic frame from guideline-sourced endocrine workup; Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: A1c: normal <5.7%; prediabetes 5.7-6.4%; diabetes >=6.5%. Fasting plasma glucose: normal <100 mg/dL; prediabetes 100-125; diabetes >=126.; Important mimics/exclusions: stress or steroid hyperglycemia, type 1/LADA versus type 2 diabetes, DKA/HHS versus uncomplicated hyperglycemia, hypoglycemia/drug effect, renal disease, infection, pregnancy, and secondary endocrine causes.

Prediabetes modifier changes treatment/disposition: Measure blood pressure; Measure heart rate; Measure current weight; Measure waist circumference; pregnancy/postpartum/lactation status; renal/hepatic/cardiac disease; allergy or drug interaction; pediatric/adult applicability

Prediabetes objective response supports narrowing, stopping, tapering, or continuing: normal <5.7%; 5.7-6.4%; diabetes >=6.5%; normal <100 mg/dL; A1c 5.7-6.4, FPG 100-125, or 2-hour OGTT 140-199 triggers prevention and surveillance.; Diabetes-range values change diagnosis.; Markedly abnormal glucose, severe hypertension, pregnancy-context concern, or cardiopulmonary symptoms changes disposition from prevention visit to urgent evaluation.; Offer intensive lifestyle intervention aiming for durable weight loss when appropriate, nutrition quality, physical activity, sleep, and tobacco risk reduction.
